message:
fix(cli,installer): eliminate stale WAL/SHM sidecar propagation during DB copy

Previously, copy_db_with_sidecars() in legacy.rs blindly copied WAL and SHM
sidecar files from source to target. These sidecars are ephemeral runtime
artifacts tied to the specific connection that created them -- copying them
alongside the main DB file can produce a target database whose journal state
is inconsistent with its page data, leading to "database disk image is
malformed" errors on first open.

Changes:
- Add checkpoint_sqlite_for_copy() that issues PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)
  before copying, flushing all WAL content into the main DB file so the copy
  is self-contained.
- Reverse sidecar handling: instead of copying source sidecars to the target,
  actively remove any pre-existing target sidecars that might linger from a
  previous copy or open.
- Mirror the same fix in install.sh's copy_sqlite_snapshot() shell function:
  best-effort checkpoint via sqlite3 CLI before cp, then rm -f target sidecars.
- install.sh: abort install if DB migration fails rather than silently
  continuing with a potentially inconsistent migrated database.
- install.sh: add fish shell support to verify_installation path resolution
  and eliminate eval-based command construction in favor of direct shell
  invocation, reducing injection surface.
- Add regression test copy_db_with_sidecars_omits_source_sidecars_and_preserves
  _main_db that verifies source sidecars are not transported and target data
  integrity is preserved.
- Update beads issue tracker (br-3kwox) with audit follow-up comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-3jz52","title":"T1.5: Add animated chart transitions for smooth data updates","description":"Charts should not jump when new data arrives. Instead, data points should smoothly transition
to their new positions. This creates a professional, polished feel.

APPROACH:
- Maintain previous and current data point positions
- On each render frame, interpolate between previous and current positions
- Use ease-out timing function for natural deceleration
- Transition duration: 200ms (12 frames at 60fps)

This applies to all chart widgets (LineChart, BarChart, Canvas heatmap).

Implementation in ChartDataProvider: add `interpolated_points(t: f64)` method that returns
data points lerped between previous and current state.

FILES: tui_widgets.rs (ChartDataProvider), tui_screens/dashboard.rs, tool_metrics.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Data point transitions animate over 200ms
- [ ] Ease-out timing function applied
- [ ] No visual glitches during transitions
- [ ] Can be disabled via env var for CI/testing

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:25:07.177790757Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:25:07.177720555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["animation","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3jz52","depends_on_id":"br-333hh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3jz52","depends_on_id":"br-3q8v0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-3jz52","depends_on_id":"br-rk4gw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":737,"issue_id":"br-3jz52","author":"LilacLake","text":"Implemented chart-transition scaffolding and wired it into active chart surfaces.

Changes:
- Added reusable `ChartTransition` helper in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_widgets.rs` with cubic ease-out interpolation, disable-motion bypass, and reset support.
- Added new unit tests for transition interpolation/disable-motion/reset behavior in the same file.
- Dashboard now animates throughput series via `ChartTransition` and honors `AM_TUI_CHART_ANIMATIONS` + reduced-motion flags (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/dashboard.rs`).
- Tool Metrics latency bar chart now renders from animated cached rows driven by `ChartTransition` (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/tool_metrics.rs`).

Validation:
- `cargo fmt --check` passes after formatting.
- Workspace `cargo check --all-targets` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` are currently blocked by pre-existing compile failures in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/cache.rs` (IndexMap import/trait-bound issues unrelated to this change set).
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:05:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3k16e","title":"R1.4: Implement TOON serializers for Agent Mail domain types (messages, agents, reservations as tabular arrays)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:48.439159076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:47:21.113670953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:47:21.113648330Z","close_reason":"15 TOON-friendly domain types in robot.rs with Serialize derives + toon round-trip tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3k16e","depends_on_id":"br-3i7fz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:02:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":738,"issue_id":"br-3k16e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R1.4: TOON Serializers for Domain Types

## What
Implement `Serialize` (serde) for all domain types that robot commands return, ensuring TOON-friendly structure. The key insight: TOON's tabular array format (`items[N]{col1,col2}: ...`) works best when arrays contain uniform structs with flat fields.

## Where
- Core model types in `mcp-agent-mail-core/src/models.rs` (if not already Serialize)
- New response types in `mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/` for robot-specific views

## Domain Types Needed
Each robot command returns a specific data struct. These must be flat and tabular-friendly:

1. **StatusData** — inbox_summary, recent_activity, anomalies[], my_reservations[], top_threads[]
2. **InboxEntry** — id, priority, from, subject, thread, age, ack_status, importance
3. **TimelineEvent** — seq, timestamp, kind, summary, source
4. **OverviewProject** — slug, unread, urgent, ack_overdue, reservations
5. **ThreadMessage** — position, from, to, age, importance, ack, subject, body
6. **SearchResult** — id, relevance, from, subject, thread, snippet, age
7. **MessageContext** — full message + thread position + adjacent summaries
8. **ReservationEntry** — agent, path, exclusive, remaining, granted_at
9. **MetricEntry** — name, calls, errors, error_pct, avg_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms
10. **HealthProbe** — name, status, latency_ms, detail
11. **AnomalyCard** — severity, confidence, category, headline, rationale, remediation
12. **AgentRow** — name, program, model, last_active, msg_count, status
13. **ContactRow** — from, to, status, policy, reason, updated
14. **ProjectRow** — slug, path, agents, messages, reservations, created
15. **AttachmentRow** — type, size, sender, subject, message_id, project

## TOON Optimization Guidelines
- Keep structs FLAT (no nested objects in tabular arrays)
- Use short field names where possible (agent not agent_name)
- Timestamps as relative strings ("5m ago") for TOON, ISO-8601 for JSON
- Boolean fields as true/false (TOON renders compactly)
- Numeric fields without units (units in column header)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 15 types derive Serialize
- Unit test: serialize each type to JSON, convert to TOON, verify parseable
- TOON output for a 5-row array uses <50% of the JSON token count

## What
Implement `Serialize` (serde) for all domain types that robot commands return, ensuring TOON-friendly structure. The key insight: TOON's tabular array format (`items[N]{col1,col2}: ...`) works best when arrays contain uniform structs with flat fields.

## Where

## Domain Types Needed
Each robot command returns a specific data struct. These must be flat and tabular-friendly:

1. **StatusData** — inbox_summary, recent_activity, anomalies[], my_reservations[], top_threads[]
2. **InboxEntry** — id, priority, from, subject, thread, age, ack_status, importance
3. **TimelineEvent** — seq, timestamp, kind, summary, source
4. **OverviewProject** — slug, unread, urgent, ack_overdue, reservations
5. **ThreadMessage** — position, from, to, age, importance, ack, subject, body
6. **SearchResult** — id, relevance, from, subject, thread, snippet, age
7. **MessageContext** — full message + thread position + adjacent summaries
8. **ReservationEntry** — agent, path, exclusive, remaining, granted_at
9. **MetricEntry** — name, calls, errors, error_pct, avg_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms
10. **HealthProbe** — name, status, latency_ms, detail
11. **AnomalyCard** — severity, confidence, category, headline, rationale, remediation
12. **AgentRow** — name, program, model, last_active, msg_count, status
13. **ContactRow** — from, to, status, policy, reason, updated
14. **ProjectRow** — slug, path, agents, messages, reservations, created
15. **AttachmentRow** — type, size, sender, subject, message_id, project

## TOON Optimization Guidelines
- Keep structs FLAT (no nested objects in tabular arrays)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Unit test: serialize each type to JSON, convert to TOON, verify parseable
- TOON output for a 5-row array uses <50% of the JSON token count

-{"id":"br-3kwox","title":"Agent Mail runtime: SQLite malformed image blocks inbox/ack/send","description":"Problem: Agent Mail CLI operations (mail send, list-acks, ensure_project) fail with 'Query error: database disk image is malformed' in /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust. Impact: multi-agent coordination is blocked. Repro: run cargo run -q -p mcp-agent-mail-cli -- mail send against this project and observe malformed image error. Needed: root-cause analysis, DB repair path, and regression tests for concurrent send/ack/list-acks.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:

- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GoldSeal","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:01:19.959782481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T23:53:58.391194709Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":740,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"VioletHarbor","text":"Initial triage findings: (1) doctor check reports database fail with 'Cannot open database'; (2) sqlite3 integrity_check on /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/storage.sqlite3 returns 'database disk image is malformed'; (3) sqlite3 integrity_check on /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/storage.sqlite3.bak returns 'ok'; (4) am doctor repair --dry-run fails early with schema init malformed-image error, so automated repair path is currently blocked.","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:03:29Z"},{"id":741,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reproduced at 2026-02-13T01:07Z while trying to coordinate session: /data/tmp/cargo-target/debug/am macros start-session --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust ... fails consistently with 'ensure_project failed: SQLite error: Query error: database is locked' across 6 retries. MCP tool calls (health_check/ensure_project/macro_start_session) also timeout (-32004).","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:07:26Z"},{"id":742,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"LavenderGoose","text":"Progress 2026-02-19: reproduced failure when DATABASE_URL is set to sqlite:///data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/storage.sqlite3. Root cause: sqlite URL parsing interprets this as relative path data/projects/... (not absolute), and that relative file in-repo is malformed; absolute /data/... file is healthy. Implemented fix in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs: DbPool::new now calls resolve_sqlite_path_with_absolute_fallback(), which switches to healthy absolute sibling only when configured relative path is malformed and absolute counterpart is healthy; explicit ./ and ../ paths remain untouched. Added regression tests resolve_sqlite_path_prefers_healthy_absolute_when_relative_is_malformed and resolve_sqlite_path_keeps_explicit_dot_relative_paths. rch validation: cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-db --lib passed remotely; rch cargo test encountered worker linker env issue (missing -lsqlite3).","created_at":"2026-02-19T21:16:29Z"},{"id":743,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"OrangePlateau","text":"Revalidated rch gate behavior while working br-25kea. Current broad db test failures remain dominated by worker environment FD exhaustion (Too many open files / os error 24), including schema/pool/query tests opening temp DBs. This appears infra-host limit related, not deterministic code-path regressions in the new concurrent-writer changes. See latest rch run: env RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db --all-targets -> 695 passed / 37 failed, failures all rooted in EMFILE.","created_at":"2026-02-20T04:15:26Z"},{"id":744,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"MagentaRiver","text":"MagentaRiver runtime gate note (2026-02-20): after latest db pool/cache changes, focused query-layer validations are green via rch (check/clippy + queries::tests 65/65), but full crate sweep on worker still fails with OS error 24 (Too many open files). Repro command: rch exec -- env RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-db --all-targets. Result: 696 passed / 37 failed; failures are dominated by EMFILE while opening temp sqlite files / Tantivy index files (pool, queries, schema, search_v3 tests). This looks like remaining remote FD-exhaustion behavior rather than deterministic logic regression in the concurrent-writer query changes.","created_at":"2026-02-20T05:05:56Z"},{"id":745,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-20 GoldSeal progress: added regression coverage for malformed relative DATABASE_URL fallback in tests/e2e/test_db_corruption.sh. Changes: (1) try_session now accepts either raw path or full sqlite URL, (2) new helper tool_call_succeeded() to distinguish non-error tool responses, (3) new Case 8 validates sqlite:///relative malformed path succeeds and relative malformed file remains untouched (proving fallback behavior). Validation: bash tests/e2e/test_db_corruption.sh passed (10 pass / 0 fail). Artifacts: tests/artifacts/db_corruption/20260220_202907/.","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:29:55Z"},{"id":746,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-20 GoldSeal follow-up: strengthened new fallback regression case to exercise full flow (ensure_project + register_agent + send_message) under malformed relative sqlite URL () while verifying the relative malformed file remains untouched. Switched check 1/1b to non-mutating open + sidecar-aware health probe (sqlite_file_is_healthy) with malformed-relative absolute fallback handling; added regression test doctor_check_uses_absolute_fallback_for_malformed_relative_database_url. rch validation: cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli doctor_check_uses_absolute_fallback_for_malformed_relative_database_url -- --nocapture => pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:00:36Z"},{"id":1104,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"DarkCompass","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up complete on CLI doctor fallback patch. Fixed remaining db_file_sanity gap for missing malformed relative sqlite path with absolute sibling fallback; added regression test doctor_check_uses_absolute_fallback_when_relative_database_path_is_missing (crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs). rch fmt check passes for mcp-agent-mail-cli. Full crate check/test currently blocked by unrelated compile errors in mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/search.rs present in shared worktree.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:38:18Z"},{"id":1105,"issue_id":"br-3kwox","author":"DarkCompass","text":"Applied second-pass hardening in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs: (1) doctor database check now preserves open/probe error detail and fallback context, (2) configured-path-missing absolute fallback now surfaces warn status, (3) db_file_sanity now emits explicit fail on sqlite_path parse errors instead of silent skip, (4) absolute fallback candidate requires regular file + explicit-relative detection via path components. Implemented fix in crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs: DbPool::new now calls resolve_sqlite_path_with_absolute_fallback(), which switches to healthy absolute sibling only when configured relative path is malformed and absolute counterpart is healthy; explicit ./ and ../ paths remain untouched.  {"id":"br-3kwox.1","title":"Reproduce request_contact DB errors and isolate failing SQL path","description":"Observed while onboarding PurpleHorizon on 2026-03-02: send_message is blocked by contact policy, and request_contact / macro_contact_handshake return generic database errors.\n\nEvidence:\n- resource://tooling/diagnostics shows contact tools failing systematically (request_contact calls=36, errors=36; macro_contact_handshake calls=47, errors=33).\n- Health diagnostics report DB pool saturation warnings (utilization 100%) and elevated tool error rate.\n- Repro via MCP tool call: request_contact(project_key='/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust', from_agent='PurpleHorizon', to_agent='MaroonBridge') returns generic DB error.\n\nGoal for this bead: instrument and reproduce the exact database error cause in contact request flow, then patch to restore handshake path reliability.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurpleHorizon","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:15:46.546382041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T23:16:58.966652509Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3kwox.1","depends_on_id":"br-3kwox","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:15:46.546382041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1068,"issue_id":"br-3kwox.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress 2026-03-02: reproduced contact-path failure via MCP tool calls. request_contact(project_key='/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust', from_agent='PurpleHorizon', to_agent='MaroonBridge') consistently returns generic DATABASE_ERROR. macro_contact_handshake also fails similarly. In-session behavior became intermittent: send_message to self previously succeeded (message ids 1168/1169) but later also returned DATABASE_ERROR, indicating broader transient DB failure surface beyond contact policy gating. Health endpoint still reports green, so failure is likely in a specific query/transaction path not captured by high-level liveness checks.","created_at":"2026-03-02T23:16:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3l1t","title":"Fix inconsistent expiry boundary in in-memory reservation filters","description":"Commit 5bd9a46 fixed the SQL boundary from 'expires_ts < ?' to 'expires_ts <= ?' but three in-memory filters still use the old strict-less-than boundary: resources.rs:3571, resources.rs:3600, reservations.rs:642. This creates a window where a reservation at exact expiry is excluded from the active list (correct) but also excluded from the expired cleanup (incorrect), creating an orphaned state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:32:05.321774571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:40:37.498071088Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:40:37.498052132Z","close_reason":"Fixed 5 inconsistent expiry boundary comparisons across resources.rs, reservations.rs, cleanup.rs, and guard/lib.rs. All now use inclusive boundary (<=) matching the SQL fix in 5bd9a46.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-3lc7f","title":"T10.8: Execute cross-platform validation matrix for all migrated native commands (Linux/macOS/Windows)","description":"## Objective
Establish an explicit cross-platform acceptance matrix for all migrated native commands so portability claims are proven, not assumed.

## Work
- Define OS matrix coverage for Linux, macOS, and Windows environments (or documented equivalent CI images).
- Validate each migrated command path (`am ci`, `am flake-triage`, `am bench`, `am check-inbox`, serve enhancements, `am golden`, share wizard, verify-live, native E2E harness).
- Stress platform-sensitive behavior: path normalization, newline/encoding differences, process spawning, lock semantics, env-file handling, and shell quoting assumptions.
- Capture deterministic per-OS artifacts and troubleshooting bundles.
- Open follow-up beads for any platform-specific gaps discovered.

## Deliverable
A reproducible cross-platform evidence set that confirms migrated native workflows are truly portable for end users.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- Matrix execution covers every migrated command surface in this epic across Linux/macOS/Windows.
- Platform-sensitive edge cases are explicitly exercised and documented (filesystem, process, network, env, and terminal behavior).
- Per-OS runs emit machine-readable evidence bundles (JSON summaries, command traces, stdout/stderr, timing, environment fingerprint).
- Any failures produce actionable follow-up beads with dependency links back to affected migration tracks.
- Final epic audit (`T10.6`) consumes this matrix as mandatory closure evidence.\n\n

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this Progress 2026-02-23 (JadeCarp):
- Claimed br-3lc7f.
- Added CI job `.github/workflows/ci.yml` -> `native-command-matrix` with required Linux/macOS/Windows runs.
- Matrix captures per-case artifacts under `tests/artifacts/cli/native_command_matrix/<os>/`:
  - `commands/<case_id>/{command.txt,stdout.txt,stderr.txt,exit_code.txt,timing_ms.txt}`
  - `environment.json`
  - `summary.json`
- Updated skip gate so `skip-guard` requires `native-command-matrix` success.
- Updated docs:
  - `docs/SPEC-script-migration-matrix.md` (new T10.8 cross-platform matrix section + artifact contract)
  - `docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md` (gate matrix + evidence map rows for cross-platform portability)
- Local validation completed:
  - workflow YAML parse check: `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml")'`
  - matrix command surface checks: `am <command> --help` for all listed matrix cases.

@@ -654,9 +654,13 @@ copy_sqlite_snapshot() {
     rm -f "$tmp_db" 2>/dev/null || true
   fi
 
+  if command -v sqlite3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    sqlite3 "$src_db" "PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000; PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+  fi
+
+  # Sidecars are intentionally omitted to avoid propagating stale WAL/SHM state.

-  [ -f "${src_db}-shm" ] && cp -p "${src_db}-shm" "${dest_db}-shm"
+  rm -f "${dest_db}-wal" "${dest_db}-shm" 2>/dev/null || true
 }
